title: rosa palustris f. inermis
intro: Rosa palustris f. inermis, also known as the sweet-scented swamp rose or the prickly swamp rose, is a shrub native to eastern North America. It is a member of the rose family and is known for its clusters of fragrant pink flowers and edible hips, which are the fruit of the rose plant. The medicinal properties of Rosa palustris f. inermis include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, and herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves can be made from the plant's flowers, hips, and leaves.
